theme: Tune the cooling curve for a fixed searcher
title: Blindfolded Blacksmith: Tuning the Quench
strategies: do-nothing / T=0: pure greedy hill-climbing, drives every trap block straight to its u=0 consolation value and never leaves; reproduces the evaluator's own baseline

single fixed-guess monotone cooling: pick one plausible T0 (informed only by public block sizes, since the hidden reward slope is never shown) and decay it once across the whole step budget with no restarts -- solves shallow traps whose barrier happens to be near the guessed scale, but a fixed guess is systematically wrong on instances whose hidden slope is far from that guess and never recovers

acceptance-adaptive compounding reheat: start from a modest temperature, and whenever a stagnation window shows both a low realised acceptance rate and no improvement in the best score, ESCALATE the temperature multiplicatively (compounding across repeated events) and resume from the best state found so far -- self-calibrates to whatever the hidden slope actually is, rather than assuming it, and gets many independent attempts to cross each trap instead of one

seeded local refinement of the escalating policy: perturb window/stagnation_window/reheat_factor/restart_mode around a working adaptive baseline and keep whichever setting yields a higher observed acceptance-rate recovery per event, deterministically, using only public fields
